raw_response:
I agree that option D is correct based on the information presented. However, adding more detail can strengthen our understanding further - while increased Total Iron Binding Capacity (TIBC) helps compensate for lower free iron levels by binding available iron more tightly, this also leads to decreased Transferrin Saturation because less of the protein-bound iron becomes effectively utilized despite being present. This interplay between TIBC elevation and transferrin saturation reduction characterizes iron-deficiency anemia accurately.

Answer Choice: D. Your agreement includes additional context about how elevated TIBC affects transferrin utilization efficiency, reinforcing why D correctly represents key aspects of iron deficiency anemia diagnosis.
